item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like some of the redacting was a little excessive, but otherwise interesting. +1 though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last log bit really creeps me out and i want to upvote, but the rest of it just seems long and confusing in comparison.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, this means a lot to me.

item.title: Re: Thanks (not)
item.content:encoded: I've had it too (not regularly though) and was surprised to find there wasn't already a similar SCP. I think a therapy bill is possibly the highest compliment I could receive for a creepy article, so… thanks? I guess?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dropping this post here to say that I really hope this article receives more attention. It deserves it. One of the few articles to ever actually give me the chills.

item.title: Thanks (not)
item.content:encoded:
This is my first ever post on this site. You have given me the willies and I don't know when I will ever sleep again and I needed to let you know.

You've taken a personal fear that took me years of scientific rationalization to get rid of and took me into full blown anxiety attack in five measly minutes.

Okay, so I'm exaggerating slightly. But seriously, I was plagued by sleep paralysis as a child and your scip made me regress into a sniveling preteen afraid to go to sleep because witches rode my back almost nightly.

Good job sir. Where do I send my therapy bill? It's the least you could do in return for this +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone who's had a acute interest in lucid dreaming, this gave me the creeps in a major way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It took until the logs to get going, but once it did it got reeeeeal creepy reeeeeal fast. The weather forecast gave me chills.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough - I did worry that the description might not be strong enough to sustain the rest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn't really interested in it until I got the the note at the end, which I liked. Neutral voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As it's fairly open-ended I'm putting it in a collapsible in case people want to come up with their own ideas.

+ show block
– hide block

The significance is hinted at in the chapter title 'Prophecies and Alternate Worlds' - reading the book causes the subjects to be able to receive information from the future, or a parallel earth, that's going through a nuclear holocaust. The information comes in varying amounts, from brief bits of attack warnings to lengthy and incoherent ramblings from traumatised survivors, and is sometimes muddled with unrelated information from a different time (e.g. the radio report that starts off as a standard weather report).

The final log is more to do with the nature of the hallucinations themselves, although I'm deliberately not elaborating on what happens when people do the things they're told not to, as I wouldn't be able to come up with anything as scary as not saying it.

That's not set in stone by any means - if other people come up with alternative explanations that make sense then I'll consider them equally valid (and potentially better).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The whole thing. More specifically what is going on during the dream sequences and their significance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there anything in particular you want to ask about, or are you confused by the whole thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meant to say thanks to MadCatUSA for their critique in the forum. Also it seems only fair to mention that the final log was heavily inspired by The_Hidden_Fapping's post on this Askreddit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not quite sure what is happening here. +1 as soon as I find out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The excerpt from the manual gave me chills. Very nice work.
